content.problemStatement:
There are NNN children and Chef wants to give them 111 candy each. Chef already has XXX candies with him. To buy the rest, he visits a candy shop. In the shop, packets containing **exactly** 444 candies are available.

Determine the **minimum** number of candy packets Chef must buy so that he is able to give 111 candy to each of the NNN children.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains two integers NNN and XXX — the number of children and the number of candies Chef already has.


Output Format
For each test case, output the **minimum** number of candy packets Chef must buy so that he is able to give 111 candy to each of the NNN children.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 111: Chef must buy 222 more packets after which he will have 202020 candies which will be enough to distribute 111 candy to each of the 202020 children.
Test Case 222: Chef does not need to buy more packets since he already has 100100100 candies which are enough to distribute 111 candy to each of the 101010 children.
Test Case 333: Chef must buy 111 more packet after which he will have 131313 candies which will be enough to distribute 111 candy to each of the 101010 children.
Test Case 444: Chef must buy 333 more packets after which he will have 212121 candies which will be enough to distribute 111 candy to each of the 202020 children.
